	214={ ### The Qianlong Emperor
		# [bookmark-1763 #304] Display him by ERA name as "Qianlong Emperor" (乾隆帝), not the
		# temple name Gaozong (廟號) or personal name Hongli (弘曆) — mirroring the 1815-start
		# shortcut that names char:224 by its era name. first_name is a literal (no matching
		# character_names loc key resolves it) and the emperor's clan name is not appended in
		# the ruler display, so the throne shows exactly "Qianlong Emperor".
		first_name="Qianlong Emperor" #era name 乾隆帝; temple name Gaozong, personal name Hongli
		family="c:CHI.fam:Aisin Gioro"  # [R2-12] link to the real Aisin Gioro family (was family_name only → GetFamily returned auto key minor_chi)
		#nickname="NICKNAME_QIANLONG"
		birth_date=1711.09.25
		father="char:209"
		mother="char:210"
		# [#329 boot-test] REMOVED death_date=1799.02.07 — a setup ruler carrying a death_date is
		# read as already-dead by the setup snapshot ("set_as_ruler ... Qianlong Emperor ... is not
		# alive"), which would auto-pick a placeholder emperor. See RUS char:144 for the rationale.
		culture="manchu"
		religion="vajrayana"
		#marry_character="char:212"
		add_trait = chinese_emperor
		no_traits=yes
		# [bookmark-1759 task #195] Apogee-of-the-Qing (1759.9.1) reigning emperor. Qianlong
		# (Hongli), age 47 in 1759, at peak vigour — the Xinjiang conquest just completed.
		# Ruler assignment moved here from char:224 (Jiaqing), who is UNBORN at the 1759 start
		# (b.1760.11.13). Era-start grants mirror the old Jiaqing block. See early_bookmark_decisions.md D5.
		c:CHI={
			set_as_ruler=char:214
		}
		add_gold=1000
		add_popularity=50
		dna=""
	}

	# [bookmark-1759 task #195] QIANLONG-ERA COURT BENCH (1759 apogee).

	# members are born in the 1760s-1780s and are unborn or infants on
	# 1759.9.1, leaving the Grand Council bench thin at the apogee start.
	# This block seeds the historically-attested Qianlong court of 1759, all
	# born well before the start (from research/QIANLONG_ROSTER_RESEARCH.md,
	# EN+ZH verified). Characters defined inside CHI={} are auto-employed as

	# trait biases each toward his historical strength. Oracle a6b4f4f3 confirmed
